art_style: fine engraving line work over muted ink wash, architectural precision meeting acoustic abstraction — standing waves rendered as visible geometry in air, the way a physicist might illustrate sound if they were also grieving
color_palette: aged verdigris, warm tallow-candle amber, deep resonance blue (the color of a bell's interior shadow), limestone off-white, cold pewter, dried-blood rust, ink black
texture: cast bronze and worn stone — the pitted surface of old metal, the smoothed edges of steps walked for two hundred years, paper that has been handled too many times
mood: precision as a form of control, warmth that has been institutionalized until it is no longer warm, the specific loneliness of a system that works perfectly and still fails people
reference_artists: Albrecht Dürer's engraving discipline meets the architectural melancholy of Piranesi, with the restrained emotional weight of Shaun Tan
cover_concept: a fourteen-year-old boy stands in a bell foundry at night, back to the viewer, looking up at a single enormous bell suspended mid-cast — still molten at its lip, glowing amber-orange against the cold stone dark. His shadow stretches toward it. The bell is not yet rung. The boy is not yet whatever he is about to become. No title treatment obscures the bell's mouth.
ornament_concept: a cross-section diagram of a bell — the kind found in an acoustician's technical manual — rendered in fine engraving line, with the standing wave pattern drawn inside it as concentric arcs. Each chapter's bell is tuned differently: the wave pattern shifts, tightens, or breaks across the book's arc
scene_break_concept: a single horizontal wave-form line, the width of the text block — clean, precise, the visual equivalent of a breath held between notes
map_concept: Cantamura drawn as an acoustic diagram rather than a conventional map — the city's districts arranged around the original forty-seven bell positions, with propagation rings showing how sound travels through the amphitheater geography. Streets are labeled with their resonant frequencies. The Academy sits at the convergence point of the most binding intervals. Veth appears at the map's cold edge, unlabeled except for a single word in small capitals: REMEMBERS. Rendered in the style of an eighteenth-century scientific instrument manual.
